channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope Francis hospitalized to treat bronchitis and perform other diagnostic tests, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was hospitalized Friday to treat a weeklong bout of bronchitis and undergo some necessary diagnostic tests, the Vatican said, confirming the latest issues with the 88-year-old's pontiff's health.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guarded optimism in India as Trump and Modi outline plans to deepen defense partnership
item.description: There is a guarded optimism among military experts in India as Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump have outlined plans to ramp up sales of defense systems to New Delhi to deepen the U.S.-India strategic relationship.
item.dc:creator: Aijaz Hussain, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vance will meet Zelenskyy amid concerns about US-Russia talks to end the war in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he thinks a Russian drone strike at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant in the Kyiv region is a “very clear greeting" from Russian leader Vladimir Putin to the Munich Security Conference.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Plane carrying Secretary of State Rubio to Europe turned around because of a mechanical issue
item.description: An Air Force plane carrying Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Senate Foreign Relations Committee chairman, Sen. Jim Risch, to Germany for the Munich Security Conference was forced to return to Washington late Thursday after developing a mechanical issue.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Spicer: China must ‘significantly deescalate’ before US can consider reducing military budget
item.description: Former White House press secretary Sean Spicer argued that China must “significantly deescalate” before the U.S. can consider reducing its military budget.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Hong Kong plans to ban substandard tiny apartments. Low-income families fear higher rents
item.description: Hong Kong plans to crack down on poor-quality subdivided flats are leaving many low-income residents uncertain about their future in one of the world’s most expensive housing markets.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US deports 119 migrants from a variety of nations to Panama
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama has received the first U.S. flight carrying deportees from other nations as the Trump administration takes Panama up on its offer to act as a stopover for expelled migrants, the Central American nation’s president said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia rejoices at Trump-Putin call as Zelenskyy rejects talks without Ukraine present
item.description: Russian officials and state media took a triumphant tone Thursday after President Donald Trump jettisoned three years of U.S. policy and announced he would likely meet soon with Russian President Vladimir Putin to negotiate a peace deal in the almost three-year war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: BARRY HATTON, ILLIA NOVIKOV and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia says US 'our main counterpart' in peace talks, sidelining Ukraine
item.description: Russia said Thursday that the United States will be its “main counterpart” in forthcoming peace talks to end its invasion of Ukraine, sidelining Kyiv in the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Russian crypto expert Alexander Vinnik returns to Moscow in Russia-US prisoner swap, reports say
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Alexander Vinnik, a Russian cryptocurrency expert who faced Bitcoin fraud charges in the United States, returned to Russia on Thursday after being freed in a swap that saw Moscow release American Marc Fogel, Russian news agencies reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum threatened to sue Google Thursday over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he wants Russia back in the G7
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he would like to see Russia reinstated in the Group of Seven (G7) major economies and suggested various actors other than Moscow were to blame for Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Russian-American ballerina 'in transit' after US says she was wrongfully detained, boyfriend says
item.description: Russian-American ballerina Ksenia Karelina, who was imprisoned in Russia after donating just over $50 to a Ukrainian charity last year, is “in transit," her boyfriend tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage's mom says daughter was forced to fake her death for a propaganda video
item.description: Orly Gilboa, mother of former Hamas hostage Daniella Gilboa, said her daughter was forced to fake her own death and was covered in debris for a propaganda video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas says it will free more Israeli hostages on Saturday as originally planned
item.description: Hamas announced on Thursday it will free Israeli hostages on Saturday as originally planned, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada’s new fentanyl czar says goal is to totally stop the drug from entering the US
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — The man appointed by the Canadian government to help stop fentanyl from entering the United States said his goal is to totally eliminate the drug crossing the border.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egypt reportedly releases details on plan to rebuild Gaza; there's no mention of 'cooperation' with the US
item.description: Egyptian sources have reportedly revealed initial details of a proposal for the reconstruction of the Gaza Strip, but it appears to omit plans to "cooperate" with the U.S. or Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he and Putin agreed to begin ‘negotiations’ on ending Ukraine war
item.description: Trump says he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin have agreed to begin "negotiations" on ending the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says American Marc Fogel freed from Russia due to 'strength' of Trump
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday that Marc Fogel, an American who had been detained in Russia since 2021, was freed because the U.S. has a "strong president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Jack the Ripper's identity revealed after DNA match, historian says
item.description: A historian believes he has determined Jack the Ripper's identity through a DNA match of a shawl found at the scene of one of his murders.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: American Marc Fogel released from Russian custody
item.description: An American teacher detained by Russia is heading back to American soil, the Trump administration announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli cabinet backs Trump’s demand for Hamas to release all hostages by Saturday deadline
item.description: Israel’s security cabinet supports President Trump’s demand that Hamas release all of its hostages by noon on Saturday, an Israeli official told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rescuers recover 1 body and search for 28 people in a landslide in southwest China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Emergency teams in China's southwestern Sichuan province raced against time Sunday to locate 28 people after a landslide triggered by rains killed one person and buried homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: One of the victims of Sweden's deadliest mass shooting called his fiancée to say he loved her
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — Wounded and bleeding, Salim Karim Iskef managed to video-call his fiancée to tell her he loved her one last time before he died this week in what was Sweden's deadliest mass shooting, The 28-year-old asked Kareen Alia to look after his mother and herself before the call ended.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation
